items.title: Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky settles FTC case with $10M payment
items.summary: The FTC order ties Mashinsky’s $10 million payment to a mostly suspended $4.72 billion judgment that can be revived over asset disclosures.

items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: Guide, deliver, repeat: the hidden driver of token performance
items.summary: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Jordan Brewer writes on the missing piece in token markets: institutional-grade investor relations. Then, Martin Burgherr breaks down how crypto markets are maturing, becoming more efficient and lower risk for institutions.

items.title: Bitcoin recovery stalls after Fed holds interest rates, citing ‘uncertainty’ in Middle East
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped under $75,000 after FOMC minutes showed the US Federal Reserve holding interest rates and expressing slight concerns over inflation and the war in Iran.

items.title: Bitcoin falls as traders cut risk ahead of FOMC: Will TradFi, spot ETF volumes bolster $70K support?
items.summary: Bitcoin price volatility tends to spike before and after the FOMC, a pattern that is playing out this week. Will institutional investor BTC buying protect the $70,000 support?

items.title: Canada proposes crypto ATM ban over scams and money laundering
items.summary: Ottawa says Bitcoin ATMs have become a key tool for scammers, as regulators move to tighten oversight of high-risk parts of the crypto sector.

items.title: Bitcoin, stocks risk 'months' of losses as Kevin Warsh Becomes Fed chair
items.summary: Bitcoin fell after each new Federal Reserve chair began work, data showed, while Kevin Warsh gave mixed signals over policy for risk assets.

items.title: Trailmark turns code into graphs
items.summary: We’re open-sourcing Trailmark, a library that parses source code into a queryable call graph of functions, classes, call relationships, and semantic metadata, then exposes that graph through a Python API that Claude skills can call directly. Install it now: uv pip install trailma

items.title: Ouch. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yield just hit 5% and bitcoin may pay the price
items.summary: Hawkish dissent within the Federal Reserve, elevated oil prices and rising long-term inflation expectations are pushing bond yields higher.

items.title: Bitcoin slides toward $75,000, ETH, SOL, XRP drop as oil hits four-year high
items.summary: Crypto sold off across the board with bitcoin down 2.1% and ether off 3.4% as Brent crude surged 7.1% to $126 a barrel on reports President Trump is being briefed on military options for Iran.

items.title: Hyperliquid’s HYPE token could be its prediction market weapon, Arthur Hayes says
items.summary: HIP-4 could become a dominant prediction-market venue because Hyperliquid users can get economic exposure to platform usage through HYPE, unlike users of Polymarket or Kalshi.

items.title: Meta rolls out stablecoin payouts for creators in Philippines, Colombia
items.summary: The latest stablecoin move comes years after Meta first dipped its toes into stablecoins through Libra in 2019, before scrapping the plans in 2022 due to regulatory pushback.

items.title: US Senator Tillis to push Senate Banking vote on stalled crypto bill
items.summary: Republican Senator Thom Tillis says the Senate’s version of the CLARITY Act has “made a lot of progress,” and it was time for lawmakers to vote on advancing the bill.

items.title: WLFI drops 14% as controversial token unlock goes to vote
items.summary: The proposal to unlock more than 62 billion WLFI tokens over four to five years has 99.95% approval but is still facing backlash on X.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Twenty One Capital rises on proposed merger with Strike and Elektron
items.summary: Tether proposed that Elektron founder and CEO Raphael Zagury serve as president of the proposed merged company, while Strike's Jack Mallers would also serve in an executive role.

items.title: US, UAE and China joint effort dismantles 9 crypto scam centers
items.summary: In another police action, European police arrested 10 people and took down three scam centers, estimated to have stolen over $58 million from victims around the world.

items.title: Bitcoin futures signal caution as long-to-short ratio signals positioning shift
items.summary: Bitcoin derivatives highlight traders’ nervous view as the Federal Reserve holds interest rates and BTC struggles to trade above its range highs. Are the bears back?

items.title: Extending Ruzzy with LibAFL
items.summary: LibAFL is all the rage in the fuzzing community these days, especially with LLVM’s libFuzzer being placed in maintenance mode. Written in Rust, LibAFL claims improved performance, modularity, state-of-the-art fuzzing techniques, and libFuzzer compatibility. For these reasons, I s

items.title: Jack Mallers' Twenty One Capital surges after majority holder Tether proposes 3-way merger
items.summary: Tether has moved to combine bitcoin treasury, mining, and financial services under one roof.
